title: Speedboat Rental Licence in Marbella Port No: 2026 Tips
html:
Planning a speedboat rental in Marbella Port No often raises the question of whether you need a licence. Many travellers opt for a licensed skippered charter, which avoids licence concerns and typically costs between €250 and €600 for a half-day outing. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators offering exactly this type of experience.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Marbella Port No?

In Spain, you need a recreational boating licence for any motorboat over 15 horsepower unless you hire a skipper. The exact requirement depends on your nationality and the boat's power; operators on BoatHire24 can confirm what applies to you. A practical option is to book a skippered boat, which requires no licence from you.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Marbella Port No?

Half-day speedboat rentals in Marbella Port No range from €250 to €600, depending on boat size and season. A shared tour on a rigid inflatable boat starts around €80 per person. Private charters for up to eight guests cost more, especially in July and August. You can find a skippered charter on BoatHire24 that includes fuel and insurance.

Where to set off in Marbella Port No

Most speedboat rentals depart from Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, the main marina in the centre of town. Other popular departure points include Puerto Banús, a glamorous harbour just west of Marbella, and the smaller marina at Cabopino. From here you can reach the coast of Benalmádena or head to the sheltered waters of Bahía de Marbella. The island of Isla de la Galera, about five nautical miles south, is a common stop for swimming and snorkelling.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), ideal for a quick trip to nearby coves; seats up to 6 people; from €250 half-day.
  2. Bowrider speedboat, good for families and watersports; accommodates 8 guests; from €350 half-day.
  3. Axopar 22, a modern, versatile boat for coastal cruising; sleeps 2 overnight; from €400 half-day.
  4. Sunseeker Predator 50, a luxury performance yacht for a premium experience; up to 12 guests; from €1,500 half-day.
  5. Quicksilver Activ 605 Open, 150 HP, a reliable and fun day boat; seats 5 people; see the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, 150 HP for hire for exact pricing and availability.

What to know before you go

The peak season for speedboat rental in Marbella Port No runs from June to September, when sea temperatures are warm and the Poniente wind often creates calm conditions in the morning. Booking in advance is essential during these months. If you hold a recognised boating licence from an EU country, you can usually rent an unskippered boat up to 20 metres, but the operator will verify your credentials. For non-EU licence holders, a temporary Spanish permit may be required.

In August, set off early to avoid the afternoon breeze; the Bahía de Marbella is usually calmest between 9 am and 1 pm.

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward: you can compare listings, read reviews, and pay securely online. The platform explains how boat rental works in clear steps, from searching to returning the vessel.

At a glance

  • Price range: €250, €600 half-day, €80 shared tour
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours), full-day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 5, 12 depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella Port No

Can I rent a speedboat without a licence?

You can rent a speedboat without a licence if you book a skippered charter, which includes a captain who handles navigation.

What is the minimum age to drive a speedboat in Marbella Port No?

The minimum age to drive a rented speedboat in Spain is 16 with the appropriate licence, but skippered charters have no age restriction for passengers.

Is a deposit required when renting a speedboat?

Most operators ask for a refundable deposit of €500 to €1,500, held on your card during the rental period.

Ready to get on the water? Browse verified boats and captains on BoatHire24, or if you own a boat in Marbella Port No, you can check availability and list it to earn income from your vessel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella Port No and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
